metadata.description: Integration of Coverage, Redundancy, and Intent alignment logic using Rule-based terminology

rubrics.description: Can the game_plan be FULLY realized using ONLY the selected rules? FAIL if the plan describes mechanics (e.g., wave spawning) but no corresponding rule is selected, or if a rule's description does not support its assigned mechanics.

Are there any rules NOT required by the implementation_plan? FAIL if a rule is selected but the plan never utilizes its functionality (e.g., 'despawn_unit' selected but no despawning logic is described).
Logic in 'Reasoning' must match the 'Logic Description' in rules without contradictions.
Strictly verify the 'No victory/defeat conditions in rules' constraint and adherence to rule count limits.
Evaluate if combined mechanics conflict (e.g., Fast Build vs. Resource Stall, or Low Damage vs. Immortality).

Are the selected decisions appropriate for the user intent and scenario? FAIL if: (1) a movement-only scenario includes construction or production decisions, (2) a combat micro scenario lacks movement decisions, (3) mutually exclusive pairs are both selected (SimpleConstruction/ConstructionWithPos, SimpleProduction/ProductionWithFactory, SimpleMovement/GroupMovement), or (4) decisions do not match what the user asked the agent to be evaluated on.
Does the game_description accurately fulfill the original user_intent? FAIL if key elements are missing or if the focus shifts (e.g., defensive intent vs offensive gameplay).
Stay focused on the Core Mechanic; avoid unrequested 'Feature Creep'.
The description must be understandable for players without needing to read code.
